project.name: ankurray.com - Subscription Feature
project.description: Full-stack subscription system with premium content gating

epics.name: Backend Infrastructure & Authentication (Spring Boot Setup)
epics.description: Set up Spring Boot API with user authentication and database

tickets.title: Spring Boot Project scaffold with Spring Data JPA, PostgreSQL driver, Gradle
tickets.description: Create a new Spring Boot 3.x project with necessary dependencies configured. Set up project structure and verify local build.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want a properly configured Spring Boot project, so that I can start implementing backend features.
acceptanceCriteria: Spring Boot project created with Spring Data JPA dependency

tickets.title: Database schema design and Flyway migrations
tickets.description: Set up PostgreSQL locally, configure Flyway for migrations, create initial schema for users, articles, subscriptions, and payments.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want versioned database migrations, so that schema changes are tracked and reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: Migrations run successfully on local database
Schema verified with psql or DBeaver

tickets.title: User entity, repository, and BCryptPasswordEncoder
tickets.description: Create User JPA entity with proper annotations, UserRepository for database operations, and configure Spring Security's BCryptPasswordEncoder.
tickets.userStory: As a system, I want User data persisted securely, so that user accounts can be created and retrieved.
acceptanceCriteria: Fields: id, email, passwordHash, firstName, lastName, role, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
Unit test: test password hashing and verification

tickets.title: JWT configuration (token generation/validation with Spring Security)
tickets.description: Create JwtTokenProvider for generating and validating JWT tokens, configure Spring Security with JWT authentication filter.
tickets.userStory: As a user, I want stateless JWT authentication, so that I can call protected endpoints with a token.
acceptanceCriteria: JwtTokenProvider class created with generateToken() method
JWT_SECRET and JWT_EXPIRATION configured in application.properties
SecurityConfig configured with JWT filter chain
Token expiration tested (valid and expired tokens)
Test: verify token generation and validation

tickets.title: Registration and login endpoints
tickets.description: Create AuthController with /register and /login endpoints. Validate email format, handle duplicate emails, return JWT token on successful login.
tickets.userStory: As a user, I want to register and login via API, so that I can authenticate and receive a JWT token.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: register new user, login with correct/incorrect credentials

tickets.title: Protected endpoint configuration and role-based access
tickets.description: Configure Spring Security to protect endpoints. Create @PreAuthorize annotations for role-based access (USER, ADMIN). Create /api/me endpoint.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want role-based access control, so that only authorized users can access restricted endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/me endpoint returns current user info (requires auth)
Test: access protected endpoint with/without token
Test: role-based access denied for insufficient roles

epics.name: Free Content Management
epics.description: Move articles from static HTML to database

tickets.title: Article entity, repository, and ArticleService
tickets.description: Create Article JPA entity, ArticleRepository, and ArticleService with business logic for fetching and filtering articles.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want article persistence and retrieval logic, so that content can be served from database.
acceptanceCriteria: Fields: id, title, slug, content, category, authorId, isPremium, createdAt, updatedAt
ArticleService with methods: findAll(), findById(), getPublicArticles(), getPremiumArticles()
Test: filter articles by premium status

tickets.title: Article API endpoints (list, detail with pagination)
tickets.description: Create ArticleController with public endpoints for listing and fetching articles. Implement pagination and access control (free users see only free articles).
tickets.userStory: As a user, I want to browse articles via REST API, so that frontend can display content dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: Free users see only isPremium=false articles
Authenticated users can see articles they can access
Response includes: id, title, slug, category, isPremium, createdAt (not full content)
Test: verify free user cannot see premium article content

tickets.title: Migrate existing 13 HTML articles to database
tickets.description: Extract content from existing HTML files in /posts directory. Parse and convert to markdown/HTML, import into database. Mark as free or premium.
tickets.userStory: As a content owner, I want existing articles migrated to database, so that content is manageable and queryable.
acceptanceCriteria: Content converted to database format (title, slug, content, category)
Categories created: Tech, Book Analysis, Personal Journey, etc.
isPremium set appropriately (most = false initially, some = true for premium tier)
All articles inserted into database via Flyway migration or manual import script
Verify article count: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM articles = 13
Spot-check 3+ articles for complete content migration

epics.name: Subscription Model
epics.description: Build subscription tracking and access control

tickets.title: Subscription entity, repository, and SubscriptionService
tickets.description: Create Subscription JPA entity linked to User, SubscriptionRepository, and SubscriptionService for checking user access.
tickets.userStory: As a system, I want subscription data persisted, so that user access rights can be determined.
acceptanceCriteria: Fields: id, userId, status, tier, startDate, endDate, stripeSubscriptionId, createdAt, updatedAt
Test: create subscription, verify active status

tickets.title: Premium access control (401/403 logic in controllers)
tickets.description: Implement access control logic in ArticleController. Return 403 Forbidden if user tries to access premium article without subscription.
tickets.userStory: As a subscriber, I want premium articles to be protected, so that only paying users can access them.
acceptanceCriteria: If isPremium && user not subscribed → return 403 Forbidden
If isPremium && subscription expired → return 403 Forbidden
If isPremium && user is subscriber → return article content
Response includes helpful error message for unauthenticated users (redirect to login/subscribe)
Test: access granted for active subscriber
Test: access denied for expired subscription

tickets.title: Stripe SDK configuration
tickets.description: Add Stripe Java SDK to build.gradle, configure API key in application.properties, create StripeConfig class.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want Stripe SDK configured, so that I can make payment API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Stripe Java SDK added to build.gradle (stripe-java dependency)
Stripe test API key used for development
Build succeeds with Stripe SDK included
No hardcoded API keys in source code

tickets.title: Payment intent creation endpoint
tickets.description: Create PaymentController with POST /api/payments/intent endpoint. Accept plan_id, return client_secret for Stripe Elements.
tickets.userStory: As a user, I want to initiate a payment, so that Stripe can process my subscription.
acceptanceCriteria: Stripe PaymentIntent created via Stripe API
Amount calculated from planId (hardcoded for now: BASIC=$10, PREMIUM=$20)
Test: invalid plan_id returns 400 Bad Request

tickets.title: Webhook handler for payment.intent.succeeded
tickets.description: Create webhook endpoint to handle Stripe payment.intent.succeeded events. Update Subscription status to ACTIVE.
tickets.userStory: As a payment system, I want to confirm successful payments, so that user subscriptions are activated.

tickets.title: Subscription plans management
tickets.description: Create Stripe products and prices for subscription tiers (BASIC, PREMIUM, ENTERPRISE). Create endpoint to list available plans.
tickets.userStory: As a customer, I want to see available subscription plans, so that I can choose the right tier.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 Stripe products created in Stripe Dashboard: Basic, Premium, Enterprise
Response includes: id, name, price, description, features
Plan features hardcoded or from database
Plans displayed on frontend checkout page

epics.name: Frontend Integration
epics.description: Update UI to use API and handle authentication

tickets.title: Login/registration pages (HTML/CSS/JS)
tickets.description: Create login.html and register.html pages. Implement form validation, API calls to backend, JWT storage in localStorage.
tickets.userStory: As a user, I want to login/register via web UI, so that I can access premium content.
acceptanceCriteria: login.html created with email & password fields
register.html created with email, password, passwordConfirm fields
Form validation (email format, password min 8 chars)
JWT token stored in localStorage after successful login
Redirect to dashboard on successful login
Error messages displayed for failed auth
Dark/light mode CSS matches existing site theme

tickets.title: Article list fetching from API
tickets.description: Modify posts/index.html to fetch articles from API instead of static HTML. Add pagination, filters by category.
tickets.userStory: As a user, I want to browse articles dynamically, so that new articles appear without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Pagination: show 5 articles per page with next/prev buttons
Display: title, category, date, preview (first 100 chars)
Article cards link to full article detail page

tickets.title: Premium paywall UI
tickets.description: Create modal/overlay for premium articles. Show 'Subscribe to read' message, link to subscription page.
tickets.userStory: As a free user, I want to see premium content is available, so that I can purchase subscription.
acceptanceCriteria: When accessing premium article without subscription: show paywall modal
Modal displays: article title, excerpt, 'Subscribe Now' button
Modal has close button (redirects to articles page)
CSS styling consistent with site theme
Test: access premium article as free user
Test: paywall disappears for subscribed user

tickets.title: Checkout flow with Stripe Elements
tickets.description: Create checkout.html page. Integrate Stripe Elements for card payment. Flow: plan selection → card entry → payment → confirmation.
tickets.userStory: As a customer, I want smooth checkout experience, so that I can easily purchase subscription.
acceptanceCriteria: Display selected plan details (name, price, features)
Payment button sends card & planId to backend
Show loading state during payment processing
Success: redirect to dashboard with 'Welcome!' message
Error: display error message and retry button
Test: complete payment flow with Stripe test card

tickets.title: Subscriber dashboard
tickets.description: Create dashboard.html showing user profile, subscription status, renewal date, download links for PDFs/repos.
tickets.userStory: As a subscriber, I want to see my subscription details, so that I know when it renews and access premium resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Display: email, subscription tier, status, renewal date
Section for premium resources (PDFs, GitHub repos)
Download link for each PDF (calls backend API)
Test: fetch and display dashboard data

tickets.title: Unit tests for services (80%+ coverage)
tickets.description: Write unit tests for UserService, ArticleService, SubscriptionService, StripePaymentService. Target 80%+ code coverage.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want unit tests, so that business logic changes don't break existing functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: UserService tests: registration, login, password validation
ArticleService tests: fetch articles, filter by premium, pagination
SubscriptionService tests: check active status, expiration logic
StripePaymentService tests: mock Stripe API calls
Test files in src/test/java following same package structure
Test naming convention: testXXX or XXXShouldYYY

tickets.title: Integration tests for API endpoints
tickets.description: Write integration tests for full API flows: register→login→browse articles→checkout→access premium. Use @SpringBootTest + MockMvc.
tickets.userStory: As a developer, I want end-to-end tests, so that complete user flows are validated.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests for premium article access control
Full workflow test: register → login → view articles → attempt premium access (403)
Mock Stripe API for payment tests
Test isolation (no shared state between tests)

tickets.title: Deployment configuration and docs
tickets.description: Create deployment docs, Docker setup, environment variables configuration. Deploy to staging environment.
tickets.userStory: As a DevOps engineer, I want clear deployment steps, so that backend is production-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md with setup instructions (clone, env vars, run locally)
Deployment guide for Heroku / Railway / AWS (choose one)
Database migrations run automatically on deployment

legendAndNotes.storyPoints: S=Small (1-2 hours), M=Medium (2-4 hours), L=Large (4+ hours)
legendAndNotes.dependencies: Other tickets that must complete first
legendAndNotes.parentBranch: Git branch where feature PR will merge: Subscription, Login, or FreeContent
legendAndNotes.version: 1.0 - Spring Boot 3.x + PostgreSQL + Stripe + JWT Auth
legendAndNotes.importInstructions:
1. Use GitHub Issues or Jira to import these tickets
2. Assign one ticket per weekend
3. Create feature branch from parent branch
4. Commit work & push to feature branch
5. Create PR to parent branch
6. After review & green tests, merge to parent
7. Repeat for next ticket
8. Final: Create PR from parent to main for release
